howItWorks.intro: This net worth calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Net Worth = Total Assets - Total Liabilities
howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: What counts as an asset?
faq.answer: Cash, savings, investments (stocks, bonds, retirement accounts), real estate, vehicles, jewelry, and other valuables. Use current market value, not what you paid.

faq.question: What counts as a liability?
faq.answer: All debts: mortgage, car loans, student loans, credit card balances, personal loans, and any other money owed.

faq.question: What is a good net worth by age?
faq.answer: A common benchmark: by 30 aim for 1x salary saved, by 40 aim for 3x, by 50 aim for 6x, by 60 aim for 8x, and by 67 aim for 10x your salary.

faq.question: Should I include my car?
faq.answer: Include it at current resale value (not what you paid). Cars depreciate quickly, so this value drops over time.

faq.question: Is the Net Worth Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

When comparing different financial products or scenarios side by side

sections.title: What Is the Net Worth Calculator?
sections.body: The Net Worth Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the financial planning space. By entering your cash & savings, investments, home value, you get instant results including total assets, total debts, net worth. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total assets right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In financial planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to manage your finances with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your cash & savings and need to find the right total assets. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Cash & Savings ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your cash & savings — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: investments, home value, other assets, mortgage balance, other debts. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your total assets, total debts, net worth. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your cash & savings before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both total assets and total debts — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to manage your finances.
If you're unsure about your other debts, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
